reviews.review_description:
Amazing app. I really enjoyed using it with music from Stoto, an artist who shares his work for free, but it would be great with any music you like.

There are three important features I really hope will be added: a shuffle/random button for songs inside a folder, a random environment option, and a proper lying-down mode so the app keeps the correct orientation when using it while lying down.

More environments would also be awesome. I would gladly pay for this if it became even more interactive with the music, because I could use it for hours. Highly recommended, but these improvements would make it much better.

developer_response.body: Lying down mode is supported :) ... Go to headset's "Settings" menu -> then "Advanced" or "Experimental" (depending Quest 3 or 2) -> then toggle "Use apps while lying down" -> then press and hold Meta button on controller when in position.

reviews.review_title: After Meta update, songs not recognized
reviews.review_description:
Was working until Meta's version 2.1. My songs are still in Music folder. Can even click on folder and play music in browser, but game no longer recognize any of them exist. 😭

Dev pointed me to music permissions in settings and fixed problem.

developer_response.body:
~~ UPDATE: Glad things working, that issue was not a problem with Mezmer and for your updated rating, thank you!  ~~
I want to help you get this resolved. I've tested with Quest 2/3 using Meta’s OS v2.1 and the app remains fully functional, with no other similar reports made. It may be an Android file-access permission that needs to be (re)toggled for the app to 'see' your folders. As a solo dev, I’ve put a lot of work/love into this app and care deeply about your experience. Please email me at ** info@fankindmedia.com ** so I can help get you back to your music and hopefully earn a rating that reflects the app itself.

reviews.review_title: I enjoy the choices but not yet 5 stars.
reviews.review_description:
I'd like the option to save defaults, starting with Trance is not great when I want to relax. 
Also more stationary visuals ,or the option to change some parts, often I want to just chill, listen to the music and forget about reality. 
I don't enjoy visuals that make me feel like I am traveling, I get nauseous (that's on me, not the app).
Can I request to remove the rock fountain in the tunnel ? I ADORE the red flame echoes when you move your hands, but feel like those little rocks will hit me in the face when I view down to relax and take time out.

I spent quite a bit of time searching for a good VR music visualiser, reading reviews, buying apps, testing them, and often asking for refunds, and none of them delivered. Mezmer is the first one that actually does what I wanted, and it does it brilliantly.

It recognised all my MP3s on the Quest 3 straight away, and the interface makes switching between my own tracks, online radio stations, and the built-in music very straightforward. I’d still like a bit more control in the future (folder browsing, sorting options, or basic track rewind/fast-forward), but even as it is, it’s miles ahead of anything else I’ve tried.

The visualisations are the real showstopper: vibrant, reactive, and fully three-dimensional. They shift, twist, and explode with colour in perfect sync with the music, so each track feels genuinely alive. You can swap visuals (mezmers) instantly, let them rotate automatically, or stick with your favourite. The interactors add another layer of fun: you can use your hands, head movement, and on-screen controls to tweak colours, filters, styles, speed, rotation, and 3D direction. You can even boost the synchronisation by moving in rhythm with the music. Each visualisation has unique controls, which keeps things fresh: you can grab things, turn wheels, push buttons, move levers, and even throw rocks into space or punch a hairy creature (in the FLOOP visualisation).

There’s still plenty for me to explore in Mezmer, but every session has surprised me in the best way. It’s the first VR visualiser that truly lets me enjoy my own music collection with the sort of visuals I always hoped for. I’d love to see new mezmers added over time, and perhaps higher-resolution options for even more detail, but what’s already here is outstanding.

A huge thank-you to the developers. This is exactly the experience I’d been searching for. Highly recommended if you want a proper music-driven VR experience on the Quest 3.

It's a visually stimulating way to enjoy music.

You're placed in one of various 3D, VR settings, each one pulsing/reacting to the music.
(You can play your own mp3 collection or just use their (really good for dance music) player.)

Most of the scenarios have a lever which allows you to affect the action to some degree, but note that it's usually affecting the way the visuals work - I mean it's not a game, just a fun way to get more in to the music.

This would be fun after smoking, but for me, I throw on a couple of wrist-wrap weights and do weightlifting movements as a fun warm up or maybe even cardio work out if I wanna give my legs a rest.

I highly recommend this app for a trippy, immersive cardio work out.
To the devs, please add more 'scenes' if possible. You could do like six or eight at a time like a DLC!

reviews.review_title: Please add a higher resolution option
reviews.review_description: Great app but really needs higher resolution on quest 3. I also notice some sort of loudness equalizer that seems to be affecting my imported mp3s. Like the quiet parts get a heavy gain boost to try to equalizerhe loudness throughout the track. I know for a fact that effect is not present when playing the same mp3 in any other way, headset or not, so I’d like the option to disable that. Also hand tracking support would be nice
a very nice music visualizer. I hope there will be more scenes and they more unpredictable in the future

Love it. It’s perfect for what I need. 
Listen to music and chill. 
If you like that then don’t think twice about buying This
Questions: Is there a way to access to music streaming platforms, like Apple Music, or can it already do that?? Didn’t see that feature yet, but maybe I missed that. I’d also love to be able to control the speed in which some of the animations flow to match the tempo of my songs. 

Thanks so much for making this.

Use Quest Game Optimizer and set the resolution to 150-200%, otherwise the pixelation/low-resolution makes it harder to enjoy.
And if you like mesmerizing experiences also try 'Realms of Flow' - a bit less interactive, yet more cinematic and with additional effects for breathing, humming etc.

developer_response.body: Thanks epicscapes! (Optimizer is an interesting tool)

reviews.review_title: Exactly what I was looking for
reviews.review_description:
One of the main reasons I purchased a Quest was for a good music visualizer - I love just sitting back and getting lost in some good tunes. Mezmer is fantastic - beautiful visuals and just a touch of interaction if you want to play a little bit. The radio stations are very diverse, and I love that I could upload my own MP3s.

A must get if you're looking for a new way to experience music.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the fantastic review, cpeak66!

reviews.review_description: Reminds me of the wave VR from back in the day. Cool music and trippy scenes, fun controllers, lots of variety. I'm looking forward to seeing how it reacts with my own music. 5 stars, could spend hours in here.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for your review, objectnormal!

reviews.review_description: For the money- amazing - deducting 1 star because there’s no jazz or classical stations- yes- I k I can upload music - but I don’t wanna hear stuff I’ve heard - both jazz and classical would be a better fit than some genres- I just have to laugh - all those geometric shapes from psychedelics have a new life !

developer_response.body: Thanks Jimbo but confused by this because both Classical and Jazz are in the radio genre listings, each with stations available.

reviews.review_description: Just buy it!
I was looking for something to help enjoy music. I can't wait to try it out with enhancements ;-)

A sensational, stunning experience. If you like visualization and music this is a must. All the categories of music channels give an infinite number of songs. If you  have your own tracks uploaded you can use those too. Don't hesitate to try this out.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review, truly appreciated!

reviews.review_description: Tried to look for dev contact details but couldn't find anything so contacting via review. I have 400+ music files in my headset music directory. I definitely enabled storage access permission. Even uninstalled and redownloaded to accept it again to be sure. Wont show any of my own music files. Could I send you some screenshots? This would be a 5 star review if I could resolve this... Please advise... EDIT: Fankindmedia provided support. All fixed now using a primary headset account to copy the music. Music now showing. Updated to 5 stars.

developer_response.body: Glad all worked out and thanks very much for your review! :)

reviews.review_description: Needs compatibility with running audio based on what the headset is already putting out. Please do not base this on microphone input
Terrible interface, not great UI. Who gives a hoot! Put in the work and you’ll get the only legit trip experience that only vr can deliver!

reviews.review_title: Good But Needs Improvement in Small Area
reviews.review_description:
It's a decent little app, the built in radio stations need better sorting and better genre scrutiny. Half the time you press one and it's in some random language without any indication in it's English name. Or you press one and it just doesn't play anything. In regards to the dev response on the error of not seeing my own audio files: I did accept file permissions. I triple checked that. I was aware that no one else was experiencing my issue it seems, but you're not infallible to errors. 

After going through all of them again the issue appears to be an incompatible audio format, M4A that I had them under, and not a more standard MP3. I'm changing my review from 2 stars to 4. You lose a point for single minded thinking and hubris over my issue.

developer_response.body:
Sounds like the prompt that allows Mezmer access to your media files was not accepted (Mezmer reads standard audio formats, you should see the permissions prompt appear right at the start and there is also a max of like 1000 files). There have been no other reports before regarding issue you mention. Please try again and accept the prompt to access media files so that Mezmer may see/read your files. It would be a shame if this review/rating is a result of the prompt not being accepted and if so, your revision here would be greatly appreciated. If more help is needed, please email: info@fankindmedia.
Update: Thanks for your adjustment and of course not infallable :), just tried to point out things in order to help resolve, glad it is working now.

reviews.review_description: I bought half life alyx , all these supposed AAA products  and yeah cool.... but nothing sold me on VR like mezmer . Must have app for any vr or music lover. Its like you are transported to riding around on vj loops with some light interactivity. For people looking to trip this has to be your holy grail. I see developer releasing more levels and stuff im sure this has to just be the start too this should be on every new vr installed . Imagine if there was an editor and we could make our own and share em that would be so cool. A+

developer_response.body: Wow, Pedro - thanks for your review and amazing comments about Mezmer! (...and perhaps more updates here or focus on something different for the future, tbd)

reviews.review_title: Best thing I've bought in VR
reviews.review_description:
I don't even listen to music that much but geometry colors and shapes to beats makes this middle-aged cynic feel something.

Shuffle is a must have feature hopefully coming soon.
An editor would be crazy .

developer_response.body: Your review got me in my feels :) Thank you, SAT!

reviews.review_description:
i love vr music visualisers. this one's the best one i've tried so far on the quest! the quality of.. well, everything about it, is a step above most others. even just the intro screen and the loading animation look good! and unlike some apps, everything's in proper stereoscopic 3D. the fact it has so many internet radio stations is really nice. i can just fire this up, pick a radio station, and zone out! of course i can also use my own music, but i prefer the radio stations so far.

the environments that this has so far are amazing! my favorites are the sci fi tunnel that makes you feel like you're flying (Eterna), and the very colorful one that's extremely flashy (Mirix). 

edit: i just spent hours being in the Eterna environment nonstop.. i cant get enough of this app, especially Eterna!

it's already very worth the price. don't hesitate to pick this up!

but i have many ideas for this app, so here come my suggestions for the dev (not complaints!):

dev, do you want to make more visualisers? there's so much potential here! like that other reviewer said, adding the ability to make your own visualisers and load ones made by other users would be amazing. but more visualisers made by you would be great too! if you want inspiration, there are a ton of amazing audio reactive visualisers in vrchat. check out Spych Boat, The Edge, Particle Hell, Laser Hell, and Audio Optics! oh and many of the custom modded maps in beat saber. Plane9 (a free PC program) is also a good inspiration. i'd personally love some more visualisers that flash powerfully to the beat, like beat saber does (where the light fills your whole world and vision). but that's just me!

i'd also love the option to turn off the interactive elements so i can just sit and watch (EDIT: it's already in the app, seems i missed it). and the ability to use hand tracking to open the app and control it (currently it asks you to use controllers)

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review, input and support, Rose! I love how passionate and informed you are about the space!

reviews.review_description:
Love all the colors and lights with the
 music!!

developer_response.body: Thanks Gman, appreciate the review!

reviews.review_description: I love this visualizer!  I just purchased it today, and spent about an hour using it.  I can tell in about 30 seconds if a trippy type app will be good or not.  This one is great.  I loved all the environments except a couple of them, that were meh. It uses VR well to give a sense of depth, and the visualizations react well to the music.  I like the added touch that I can interact with each environment in a different way.  The only improvement I can think of right now, is to allow audio input to the microphone, so I can play Spotify in the room, and have it interact with Mesmer.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the great review, Mike!

reviews.review_description: I am looking forward to publishing my original song that has been enhanced by this easy to use app. Thanx.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review, JayaJai and awesome you’re making your own music! ~ If showcasing songs on YouTube, mentioning use of Mezmer would be appreciated :)

reviews.review_title: I would rate this as a top 5 app
reviews.review_description: So I normally don’t write reviews, but this one warrants a review. I was initially skeptical on buying this app because of the 5 star rating (thinking too good to be true) but this app is amazing. The internet radio stations available offer amazing songs I’ve never heard before (have a drink or something while you sit back and relax). This is great to chill and wind down after a long day. You only get a few scenes to play with, but each one has different filters you can apply to change the entire look. I wish the developer would have left this open for more scenes like in game purchases, but I’d be happy with future updates too. Anyways, yes it’s 5 stars. Yes it’s amazing. Yes you should buy it. Well worth its price.

developer_response.body: Thank you, Lionel ~ Your amazing review and interest in sharing it is deeply appreciated!

reviews.review_description:
This is an app I return to almost daily to listen to music and watch matching visuals and interact with the visuals. l Love it!
Some feedback:
Dancing: Embers stage has visuals trailing you hands, great for dancing. Would be great to have them on most stages. 
Buttons: Like on Blendra stage, easily accidently hit if you are moving hands or dancing. 
Speed control: Option in all tunnel visuals.
Resolution: Would be great to have the option for higher resolution on Quest 3.
Add ons: Would pay for stage add on packages.

developer_response.body:
Thank you, FunkeXMix for your review and almost daily use of Mezmer! 
UPDATE: Added a feature to hide/show interactors in scenes, so now you can dance away!

reviews.review_description: Good price

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review, Arc!

reviews.review_description: This is an EXTREMELY nice visualizer with a TON of features at a ridiculously low price.  There are 10 scenes (each of them is interactive) and you can apply 6 different color/FX filters on each scene.  The app has ample features including an original soundtrack, a freaking internet radio player (?!?) and the ability to use your personal audio files stored on your Quest.  Are there ways to improve this app?  Yes, but even in this early iteration it is extremely solid and beautiful.  I won't even waste time listing any cons--at less than $2 Mezmer is a steal.  It's a beautiful little app and if you're at all interested after watching the store's video then just buy it.

developer_response.body: Much thanks to you, Doc... for the informative review & for posting the review on SQ site as well - that really helps out!

reviews.review_description: A totally awesome experience! Go through your favourite music in different environments,  for me it's perfect to relax or just enjoy listening to the music with no annoying movements like in beat saber. Get this NOW!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks Jorge, for sharing your experience! Mezmer is in good hands with you.

reviews.review_title: THIS IS WHAT I'VE BEEN LOOKING FOR!
reviews.review_description:
VISUALS MATTER. Often, at the end of the day, I just want to plug into another world and drift away. As much as I've tried doing that with apps like Tripp and Float VR, their chonky-chonky graphics just distract me right out of the moment. (Although they are also good in many other aspects.)

And then Mezmer comes along to show what can actually be done with gorgeous, smooth, imaginative, and interactive visuals! WOW! And an embedded genre-oriented radio to boot? DUDE! I cannot wait to see what else is up this developer's sleeves! Get this app and support their work!

Would love to have some guided meditations (or embedded access to an app like Insight Timer), but for now, this will suffice nicely! Great work!

developer_response.body: Thank you for such high praise and the suggestions, JW! Dude, you Rock! (or Jazz? or Trance? or Classical? or … :)

reviews.review_description: This app is feature packed. There are lots of scenes with some interactivity. You get the option to listen to the included music, imported music, and internet radio stations! For the price there is no reason not to buy this. A++

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind review, Kniqqs! A++

reviews.review_description: Great Environments! Good Sound to Effect Movement. Advice: Please create a Spotify Playlist Functionality! :)

developer_response.body: Thank you for the great review and input, empariyon! – Much appreciated.

reviews.review_description: Will more content be added?

developer_response.body: Thank you, Aaron! I really appreciate your review. ~ Many factors to consider/balance as a solo dev on this… while more content may be possible (I have a lot more ideas & some prototypes), can’t guarantee anything yet.

reviews.review_description: 🙂🙃🙂 NEW LEVEL FOR THE GAME FEEL 🙂🙃🙂

developer_response.body: :) for the :)s and leaving a review, Mogly!

reviews.review_description: I recently mentioned this on the Reddit Oculus section in the context of this kind of app, as nobody else had mentioned it.  Three people immediately said they hadn't heard of it and would buy it right away, having seen the video.  Well, I will continue to plug it where appropriate because I think it's the best of its class - and I can't believe how imaginative the developers are, and the quality and skill of the implementation.  I guess the only thing I'd like would be fully featured web radio access rather than the preset stations, but I suspect that would be another whole thing - and probably not cheap...

developer_response.body: Peter, thank you for the fantastic support and spreading the word about Mezmer! Much appreciated :)

reviews.review_description:
I found the music visualizer of my dreams, but certainly also the one of your dreams!
An audio-visual journey like no other. Surprising and interactive visual effects. Three sources of music that I will describe briefly.
1) Demo tracks included with Mezmer. Fantastic, they stick perfectly to the different paintings. That would be enough and yet...
2) Thematic web radios. A source of very high quality non-stop music to travel to infinity.
3) And to please everyone and knock out all other app of the genre, you can listen to YOUR MUSIC, by downloading it to the music folder of the headset from your computer.
A word about VISUAL EFFECTS. Real artistic paintings that distort to the sound of music. Often with an interactive touch such as changing altitude in one painting, controlling a drone in another, rotating, moving away, by manipulating levers or beautiful light orbs.
CONCLUSION
My best purchase, because this one touches me in the essential, the emotional.
It's not a game, it's not a movie, it's just a gem. An experience to live, now and in VR. An experience that you will want to discover and share with your closest friends. Just like a gift, and what a gift!
Give yourself this gift, be nice to yourself!
A WORD TO THE DEVELOPER
Only one word. THANK YOU

developer_response.body: Wow… THANK YOU for the gift of such a review, Majerik. Your words really hit me and to know Mezmer reached someone like this is quite meaningful/appreciated.

reviews.review_description: Just amazing!

developer_response.body: Amazing of you to leave the review – much appreciated!

reviews.review_description:
Wow, a great app to chill out and get in the zone. 
Interactive and lots of features. 
Show the love!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the lovely review, Go. ~ Love is all, music is love!

reviews.review_description: Great, lively, fun music (just did the built-in songs)! Scenes are neat - but I want more (I "blew" through them all in one go). Worth the couple bucks.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review, Dani! The discounted launch price will likely change soon (also assessing additional content).
